mneomics: Indicates separation, to split something
origin: 八 represents the original meaning “to separate or distinguish” using two separated strokes. It was borrowed for its pronunciation (i.e., via sound loan) to represent the number eight.

mneomics: person is holding or covering something
usage: grouping or enclosing smth (derived from 包)
origin: 勹 depicts a person bending over, as if to lean forward over and rest their weight on an object. The original meaning is to lean forward over and rest one’s weight on an object.

mneomics: cowrie shells were used as currency in ancient times; two halves of a sea shell; animal that crawls in and out of it's shell
origin: 貝 is a picture of a cowry shell. Many ancient cultures used shells as currency. 贝 replaces 貝 in Simplified Chinese via regularization of cursive. In some characters that use the radical 貝 in the traditional form, 贝 is also used for simplification.

mneomics: possibly a reduction of 仌
origin: 冫 depicts metal plates. The original meaning “metal plates” is related to 呂. This meaning can still be seen in 冶 “to smelt” and 凝 “to condense”. Later it was borrowed to mean “ice”.

mneomics: grass are small plants growing/sprouting side by side
usage: broad term, general concept of plants
origin: 艸 is composed of two 屮 (non-tree vegetation) components. The character originally represented newly sprouted vegetation (same as 木 and 屮) and only later excluded trees. Mostly used in component form 艹.

radical: component-only form of KangXi radical Nr. 140: grass
mneomics: grass are small plants growing/sprouting side by side
usage: broad term, general concept of plants
origin: 艹 is a component form of 艸, which is composed of two 屮 (non-tree vegetation) components.

mneomics: Sprouts growing from the ground (like herbs)
origin: 屮 is a picture of newly sprouted vegetation. Before, this included both smaller, non-tree plants as well as trees (屮, 艸, and 木 wer used interchangeably). Later, it came to mean only non-tree vegetation.

english: lower form of animal life ( insects, insect larvae, worms, etc)
mneomics: mosquito planting it's stinger 中 in your revealed skin
origin: 虫 depicts a venomous snake, which is also its original meaning. 虫 was the original character for 虺, but was used (as a component) to write “insect” (traditional: 蟲) as early as the Qin Dynasty. In Simplified Chinese, 虫 replaces 蟲 by omitting two of three 虫.

radical: component-only form of KangXi radical Nr. 162: walk, halt
origin: 辶 is the component form of 辵 with the original meaning to walk down a street. The components 彳 and 止 merged over time.

measure_word: a trad. unit of length (尺/市尺=10寸/市寸,  市寸=3.3cm=1.3in)
mneomics: A thing 丶 which is smaller than the hand. The spot one inch below the wrist where the pulse is measured.
usage: actions done by hand (variant of 又)
origin: 寸 depicts a hand with a mark below the wrist, around the area where the pulse is taken, which is also the place where ancient Chinese medicine practitioner cuts cut the veins. Originally the character referred to that spot on the wrist.

mneomics: A man with arms and legs spread, trying to convey something large
usage: smth. big, large, strong, old, etc.
origin: 大 depicts the front view of a standing adult person. Originally, the character meant adult man, as in opposition to 子, and by extension to also mean big. Later, the character for adult man 夫 was derived from 大.

mneomics: a chinese cleaver (squared) knife that is held horizontally
usage: actions undertaken with sharp items like cutting
origin: 刀 is a picture of a knife. The lower/right part is the handle, and the upper/left one is the blade. When it appears as a component on the right side of a character, it is written 刂. Note: In ancient times, there was an old coin shaped like a knife (刀币), which was based on a bronze tool (削). This brought the meaning of money to the character of 刀 (US Dollar 美刀).

radical: component-only side form of KangXi radical Nr. 18: (standing) knife
mneomics: cuts with a knife
usage: actions undertaken with sharp items like cutting
origin: 刂 is a component form of 刀, which is typically written on the right side of the character.

radical: component form of KangXi radical Nr. 9: man [rén]
components: 臼 [xìn]: empty: corruption of 囟
儿 [rén]: form: a person's body (人)
mneomics: two legs of a person; child with up-raised hands wanting to be picked up
usage: having to do with people (as variant of 人)
origin: 儿 depicts the side view of a person standing. It has two different meanings stemming from different origins (人 and 兒). 儿 is a component form of 人 and in that context NOT a stand-alone character. The traditional form 兒 is composed of 臼, a corruption of 囟, and 儿. The sinplified character is also 儿, which is a modern stand-alone.

mneomics: mirror image that shows opposite forms; knuckles pressed together, ready to oppose everyone in their way
origin: 非 depicts two people with their backs to each other. The original meaning is to go against or oppose.

mneomics: Represents steps coming down from a city, or possiby a wall
origin: 阜 is an abbreviation of 𨸏, which is a depiction of hills, indicating the original meaning of a hill. The component form 阝 only appears on the left side of a character.

radical: component-only left-side form of KangXi radical Nr. 170: mound (can not find examples) [fù]
component-only right-side form of KangXi radical Nr. 163: city [yì]
mneomics: steps
origin: 阝 has two origin stories. Based on which side the component is located in the character, a different meaning is placed on it. When on the left side, it is derived from 阜 (mound). On the right side it  stems from 邑 (city).

mneomics: sheild
origin: 干 depicts and originally means a kind of instrument used for hunting or for military purposes. 干 is also used to abbreviate the characters 乾 and 幹 in simplified Chinese.

radical: simplified = KangXi radical Nr. 51: shield, dry
mneomics: at dawn 倝 when the sun comes up things begin to dry 乙
origin: The whole character of 乾 is used for its sound to express the original meaning dry (the opposite of wet). 干 is also used to abbreviate the character 幹 in simplified Chinese.

radical: simplified = KangXi radical Nr. 51: shield, dry
mneomics: farmer 人 holding his tool 干 watching the sun 日 rise 倝 behind a tree trunk 幹
origin: 幹 gàn was originally composed of 倝 and 木. The original meaning is “an upright post used when building earthen walls”. Later, 木 was corrupted via phoneticization into 干 gān, which is also the simplified Chinese form.干 is also used to abbreviate the character 乾.

mneomics: house on a hill; a high building
origin: 高 depicts a tall building, hence the original meaning of tall or lofty.

radical: Simplified form 广 is identical to KangXi radical Nr. 53: dotted cliff [yǎn]
mneomics: house on a hill with a wide/broad view of numerous things
origin: 廣 shows a large hall with a roof, but no walls. The radical 广 hints towards it being a simple building with missing walls. In Simplified Chinese, 广 replaces then 廣 by omitting 黃.
It looks like the suzhou numeral one

mneomics: represents a sheath of grain with a sagging head
origin: 禾 is a picture of a millet plant with its ears drooping under the weight of its mature grain. The original meaning is therefore grain or millet.

mneomics: a fire with sparks
usage: fire and actions related to fire like cooking
origin: 火 is a picture of fire, hence the original meaning of fire or flame. As a component on the bottom of a character, it is often written 灬.

radical: component-only form og KangXi radical Nr. 86: fire
mneomics: a fire pit
usage: fire and actions related to fire like cooking
origin: 灬 is a component form of 火, which is a picture of fire.

variants: 凡 凭 凰 凱 凳
mneomics: a table
origin: 几 depicts a table. In simplified Chinese it replaces 幾 [jǐ]

radical: simplified form is identical to KangXi radical Nr. 16: table
mneomics: how many things can fit on such a small table
origin: 幾 depicts a person (人) trapped in silk ropes (𢆶) being cut free by a spear (戈), indicating the original meaning “great danger”. Its modern meaning “a few; how many” is via sound loan.  In simplified Chinese it is replaced by 几.

radical: simplified form is KangXi radical Nr. 147: see
relatives: 覬 視 親 覺 覽
mneomics: a man with an eye for the head; a person looking through a window
usage: attirbutes and actions attributed to perception, seeing, and inspection
origin: 见 depicts a person (儿) with an enlarged eye (originally 目, now simplified), emphasizing the original meaning to see. 见 replaces 見 in Simplified Chinese.
The character shares the same form as 冂 [táng] in the modern script. https://zi.tools/zi/冂

measure_word: things with mouths (family members, domestic animals, cannons, wells, etc)
mneomics: an open/smiling mouth, where food and words enter and get out
usage: things done with one's mouth: speaking, eating, drinking, etc.
origin: 口 depicts a person’s open mouth.

english: administrative unit between the township zhèn [镇] and neighborhood lín [邻]
measure_word: (ancient) measurement of length  (= 500 m = 150 zhang (市丈)
radical: identical to simplified form of 裡 [li] and 裏 [lǐ]
mneomics: in our neighborhood there are rice fields 田 and a cemetery 土
origin: 里 is composed of a field (田) and a pile of earth (土), indicating the original meaning “a place to live”. A place that can be farmed is suitable for living. The character 里 is used in simplified Chinese to replace 裡 and 裏.
茻 is only found as a radical. It probably means numerous grass or weeds. In ancient character forms, the components 茻 and 艹 were used interchangeably dud to similar meaning.

english: entrance (of a building, vehicle or vessel)
mneomics: a pair of swinging doors
origin: 门 is an abbreviated form of 門. It depicts a double-sided door.

mneomics: rice growing in a paddie, the horizontal line is the water line
origin: 米 depicts grains of rice.

radical: aka 秃宝盖 [tūbǎogài] or 平宝盖 [píngbǎogài]

mneomics: The dot on top may represent a chimney, the edges eaves.
usage: things related to houses and buildings
origin: 宀 is a picture of a house, which is the original meaning. In comparison to 广, this hous probably has all four walls.

mneomics: a willow tree
origin: 木 depicts a tree with the upper part resembling a branch and the lower part resembling a root.

radical: identical to component form 罒 of KangXi radical Nr. 122: net
mneomics: an eye with iris/pupil, topic is something that catches the eye
usage: eyes and what you use them for (look, inspect)
origin: 目 depicts a human eye. As a component, it is often written 罒 (identical to component form of 网.

mneomics: looks like a woman sitting down with legs crossed
usage: having to do with people in general
origin: 女 is a picture of the side view of a woman in a kneeling-sit position. It is unclear whether the ancient character shows the woman's arms folded over her chest or her breasts. The original meaning is woman.

mneomics: a man blowing air from his mouth, the spirit is floating over the water like steam
usage: gas (as opposed to a liquid or solid)
origin: The ancient character for a cloud is 气. Originally, 氣 used sound loan from 气, while the meaning of giving or serving food was connected to the other compound 米. Later, the character 氣 was replaced by 餼, and 气 was replaced by 氣. In Simplified Chinese, the ancient form of 气 is used to represent 氣.

mneomics: person standing wide legged
origin: The ancient Chinese character  for 人 resembles a human standing with his hands hanging down on his side.

radical: component-only side form of KangXi Nr. 9: man
mneomics: a man facing left
origin: 亻depicts the side view of a person standing.

mneomics: The sun tells what time of day it is. Japan is the land of the rising sun.
origin: 日 depicts the sun. The character was originally a circle, but because it's not easy to inscribe on oracle bones, the character may have been changed to a square shape. The dot in 日 is to avoid confusion with 囗.

mneomics: Flesh cut into pieces. After eating the meat, only the (wishing) bones remain
origin: 肉 is a picture of a piece of meat. The original meaning of the character is the meat of birds and other animals. When it appears as a component on the left-hand side of a character, it is written 񰀷 (looks identical to 月).

mneomics: A small wedge used to hold a door open by lodging it between the door and the floor
The open door of a tent
origin: 入 depicts a wedge, indicating the original meaning “to enter” .

mneomics: a mountain with three peaks
origin: 山 depicts a mountain.

mneomics: Trimming a tree with (five) branches to make to grow new buds of life
origin: 生 depicts a plant 屮 sprouting from the ground, hence the  original meaning “to grow, to emerge”.  The ground was originally as depicted as a horizontal line 一, and later changed to 土 (after a decorative stroke was added).

components: 艮 [gěn]: empty: 艮 replaced 皀 (grain basket)
mneomics: a good 良 restaurant has a peaked roof 񃹡 (the lower two strokes are chopsticks)
origin: 食 depicts a mouth (亽) over a bamboo basket used to hold grain (originally 皀; now replaced by 艮), indicating the original meaning “to eat”.

radical: component-only form of KangXi radical Nr. 184: eat
components: 艮 [gěn]: empty: 艮 replaced 皀 (grain basket)
mneomics: a mouth eating a piece of noodle
origin: 饣 is a component form of 食. 食 depicts a mouth (亽) over a bamboo basket used to hold grain (originally 皀; now replaced by 艮), indicating the original meaning “to eat”. When 食 appears on the left-hand side of a character, it is abbreviated to 饣. 饣 replaces 飠 in Simplified Chinese via regularization of cursive.

mneomics: kneeling by a stream of water
usage: actions involving liquids (like pouring, flowing, swimming, etc)
origin: 水 depicts flowing water. As a component, it is more often written 氵or  氺.

radical: component-only form of KangXi radical Nr. 85: water
mneomics: water droplets
usage: actions involving liquids (like pouring, flowing, swimming, etc)
origin: 氵 is a component form of 水, which depicts flowing water.

german: 6. Tier im chin. Tierkreis - Schlange, Jahr der Schlange
mneomics: a sanke
origin: 巳 depicts a snake-like reptile.  It was borrowed very early via sound loan as one of the Earthly Branches.

radical: identical to KangXi radical Nr. 13: wide
origin: 冂 depicts the hall of a building. It is the original form of 堂.  The character shares the same form as 冂 [jiōng] in the modern script.

mneomics: a rice paddy,  the field is equally ino four parts
origin: 田 depicts a field with crisscrossed footpaths.

radical: identical to variant form 𤣩of KangXi radical Nr. 96: jade
mneomics: a king with his crown and sitting on his throne
origin: 王 depicts an axe blade, a symbol of power and authority, hence the original meaning of the highest ruler.

mneomics: The circle/box represents a wall surrounding something, for example, a house
origin: 囗 depicts the borders of a city.

mneomics: A desk with ink on it for writing and studying. Signs of culture are writing and literature
origin: 文 depicts a human body with linear, tattoo-like markings on it. The word was originally used to refer to the tattoos painted on a body. Later, it referred to symbols in general and was extended to also mean writing.

mneomics: depicts the slanted moon, representing evening
origin: 夕 depicts the moon at the time of its rising. The original meaning is when the moon rises at sunset in the evening. The characters 月 and 夕 were originally both in the form of a moon, with or without a point in the middle. Later on, a point in the moon shape was used to differentiate between moon (月 with point) and eve (夕 without point).

mneomics: split 八 and make it small
origin: 小 uses three small strokes to represent small pieces, which some people believe represented sand grains.

radical: component-only form of KangXi Nr. 61: heart
mneomics: The human torso is where the heart is. What touches the heart influences mind and soul
origin: 񁢉 is a component form of 心, which is a picture of a heart. When it appears as a component on the left side of a character, it is written 忄.

mneomics: The human torso is where the heart is. What touches the heart influences mind and soul
origin: 心 is a picture of a heart. The ancients believed that emotions are born in the heart and that the human mind is also dominated by the heart. When it appears as a component on the left side of a character, it is written 忄. On the bottom, it is sometimes written 񁢉.

radical: component-only left-side form of KangXi Nr. 61: heart
mneomics: The human torso is where the heart is. What touches the heart influences mind and soul
origin: 忄 is a component (left side) form of 心, which is a picture of a heart. On the bottom, it is sometimes written 񁢉.
言 was originally 舌 (tongue) with an added mark, possibly indicating movement of the tongue. Its original meaning is “spoken language.”

radical: simplified component-only form of KangXi radical Nr. 149: speech
mneomics: button that says i for information that speaks words when pressed
origin: 讠 is a component form of 言, which was originally 舌 (tongue) with an added mark, possibly indicating movement. Its original meaning is speech. In Simplified Chinese, 讠 replaces 言 when it's placed on the left side, not when it appears on the bottom.

radical: KangXi radical Nr. 53: dotted cliff, house on a cliff
mneomics: house on a hill with a wide/broad view of numerous things
usage: NOT a standalone character (in Traditional Chinese)
origin: 广 is a picture of a building or a hut. It is a simpler housing than 宀, probably because it is missing a fourth wall (on the right side). In Simplified Chinese, 广 [guǎng] replaces 廣 by omitting 黃.

english: pr. yāo for greater clarity when spelling out numbers digit by digit.
dict_entries: 一二三四五六七八九十
mneomics: one thing
origin: 一 depicts the original meaning “one” via a single horizontal line.

english: second of the ten Heavenly Stems (天干)
german: der zweite Himmelstamm des chinesischen Kalenders
origin: 乙 depicts a small stream of water.

radical: component-only right-side form of KangXi radical Nr. 163: city [yì]
component-only left-side form of KangXi radical Nr. 170: mound (can not find examples) [fù]
mneomics: a flag that marks a border between countries/districts
origin: 阝 has two origin stories. Based on which side the component is located in the character, a different meaning is placed on it. When on the left side, it is derived from 阜 (mound). On the right side it  stems from 邑 (city).

english: and (used in a mixed fraction such as one and three fourths)
mneomics: right hand fingers were chopped off twice (again and again)
usage: things done by man or with one's hand (similar to 手)
origin: 又 is a picture of a right hand with three fingers. The original meaning is "right hand". Later, the character 又 was borrowed as 再 or 更.

mneomics: precious objects 丶 of a king 王
origin: 玉 depicts three pieces of jade strung together, hance the original meaning of jade. When it appears as a component on the left-hand side, 玉 is written 𤣩.

components: 一 [yī]: empty: emphasize the person’s head
兀 [wù]: form: a person with the head emphasized
mneomics: The head of a person is the most important (primary) part of the body. It is essential for everything.
origin: 元 indicates the original meaning “a person’s head” by drawing a person with the head emphasized (兀), then adding a mark (一) next to the head.  Today it is used as a shorthand (non-official) form of 圆 due to the identical sound.

radical: identical to component-only form 񰀷 of the KangXi radical Nr. 130: meat
mneomics: the moon is every month in the same position
origin: 月 depicts the (crescent) moon. Based on the lunar cycle of waxing and waning, the character 月 later came to mean the month of the year in the calendar. Originally,  月 and 夕 were indistinguishable. Later on, to seperate them into two different characters, a point in the shape of the moon was used to represent 月.

mneomics: A baby with wailing arms and a big head
usage: life and a child's role in the family
origin: 子 depicts a child, which is the original meaning.

radical: identical to KangXi radical Nr. 39: child [zǐ]
mneomics: A baby with wailing arms and a big head is a small thing
origin: 子 depicts a child, which is the original meaning.
